title: Wide Entryway Console with Doors and Open Shelves
summary: A wide plywood console combining a lower cabinet behind two hinged doors with open shelves above — distinct from this catalog's simple open-shelf console, which has no doors at all.
description:
This console splits its storage into two zones: an enclosed lower cabinet behind a pair of hinged doors, and open shelving above where things can sit out in view — a genuinely different layout from this catalog's plain open-shelf console table, which has no doors anywhere. Rip the plywood into consistent-width strips before cutting any parts, then assemble the carcass with pocket holes at every joint except the center divider, which gets glued into position rather than pocket-screwed since it lands mid-span rather than at a corner.

The face frame's top piece overhangs the front and sides by 3/4" — a deliberate reveal that gives the console a finished cap line rather than a flush plywood edge on top. **Any additional open shelves above the doors need to be at least 1/2" narrower than the carcass itself**, since that clearance is what lets the closed doors swing past the shelf edge without binding — a detail easy to miss if you're adding shelves as an afterthought rather than planning for them from the start.

Build the door frames with pocket holes, then cut the door panel plywood slightly larger than the frame opening and nail it to the back — oversized on purpose, so the panel fully covers the frame's back opening with no gap at the edges. Hardware goes on last: hinges to hang the doors, then knobs and magnetic clasps to keep them closed.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood into consistent strips.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Overhangs the front and sides by 3/4".
Cut to fit your assembled base.
Cut slightly larger than the door frame opening.

steps.title: Rip the plywood and build the carcass
steps.body: Rip the plywood on a table saw or with a circular saw into 15-3/4" wide strips, then cut the carcass pieces from those strips. Assemble with three 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws per joint, except the center divider — glue it into position in the center of the upper shelf opening.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame on a flat, level surface with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. The top piece overhangs the front and sides by 3/4". Nail the completed face frame to the carcass with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Any additional shelves must be at least 1/2" narrower than the carcass to clear the doors.

steps.title: Add the base
steps.body: Measure and cut the side base pieces and nail to the sides with 1-1/4" brad nails. Measure and cut the front base piece and nail to the front.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Cut the back panel from 1/4" plywood and nail to all edges on the back of the cabinet with glue and 3/4" brad nails.

steps.title: Build and install the doors
steps.body: Assemble the door frame pieces with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Cut the plywood door panels slightly larger than the opening and nail to the back with 3/4" brad nails and glue. Install the doors with hinges. Add knobs or pulls and magnetic clasps.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and finish with primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Wide entryway console with cabinet doors below and open shelves above
